external.title: In Indonesia, deforestation is intensifying disasters from severe weather and climate change

record.text:
Roads turned to murky brown rivers, homes were swept away by strong currents and bodies were pulled from mud during deadly flash floods and landslides after torrential rains hit West Sumatra in early March, marking one of the latest deadly natural disasters in Indonesia.
#climatechange #environment

value.text:
Nearly every climate denial argument I’ve ever heard falls into one of these five categories.

1 - it’s not real
2 - it’s not us
3 - it’s not bad
4 - it’s too hard/costly to fix
5 - it’s too late 

And you know which is growing the fastest these days? The last one 😳

Over my lifetime, I've watched a very significant swing of opinion on nuclear energy being something that will annihilate humanity during the Cold War, to something whose waste devastates our beautifu...
external.title: Can nuclear energy ever be good?

record.text:
Been thinking about the atom, the arc of environmentalism, and my relationship to social media.

The tl;dr is that there's no ethical extraction under capitalism for any energy tech, I don't have all the answers on ethical extraction, but it wouldn't hurt to touch grass for a bit.

#ClimateChange

images.alt:
Europe is warming up at twice the global average, leading to deadly heatwaves and severe flooding, according to the European State of the Climate (ESOTC) report for the year 2023, released Monday.

According to the report, temperatures in Europe are rising 2.3 degrees Celsius (or 4.1 degrees Fahrenheit) above preindustrial levels, according to a five-year average, compared to 1.3 degrees Celsius (roughly 2.3 degrees Fahrenheit) globally. The report, jointly issued by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) and the United Nations’ World Meteorological Organization, covers the year 2023, which was the second warmest year on record for Europe. Parts of southern Europe experienced between 60 and 80 days of “strong heat stress,” with southern Spain being hit the hardest with over 80 days of “very strong heat stress.”

record.text:
#ClimateChange: Europe’s warming up at nearly twice the global average, says new report

2023 brought extreme heatwaves, severe flooding, and its largest ever recorded wildfire, according to the European State of the Climate #ESOTC report for the year 2023.

www.theverge.com/2024/4/22/24...

images.alt:
Europe is warming up at twice the global average, leading to deadly heatwaves and severe flooding, according to the European State of the Climate (ESOTC) report for the year 2023, released Monday.

According to the report, temperatures in Europe are rising 2.3 degrees Celsius (or 4.1 degrees Fahrenheit) above preindustrial levels, according to a five-year average, compared to 1.3 degrees Celsius (roughly 2.3 degrees Fahrenheit) globally. The report, jointly issued by the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) and the United Nations’ World Meteorological Organization, covers the year 2023, which was the second warmest year on record for Europe. Parts of southern Europe experienced between 60 and 80 days of “strong heat stress,” with southern Spain being hit the hardest with over 80 days of “very strong heat stress.”

images.alt: 'I felt this was an abuse of power': the climate activist who took on the law and won

record.text:
Our new offices have CO2 monitors built into them, so I started to note how low I could get the level after having a meeting by opening doors and windows.

I kept on thinking "Wow that's high, I should be able get below 400ppm at least!", then realisation slowly dawned on me... #ClimateChange

Shifting to plant-rich #diets, esp. in the North, is key to address #climatechange, restore #ecosystems, protect #biodiversity and create a just & resilient #foodsystem. Making the food system more #sustainable is largely a political & power-related issue: doi.org/10.3389/frsu...

record.text:
#ClimateChange is the biggest global threat to coral reefs. 
Climate change leads to a warming ocean. It causes thermal stress that contributes to coral bleaching and infectious disease. 

oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/coralr...

NOAA scientists warn of unprecedented bleaching across oceans, emphasizing the urgent need for global action. At the Undivided Project we take the effort to fight such battles. Join us in the fight by following us! Check the NOAA article here - www.noaa.gov/news-release...

#Climatechange
